description_long: This is a vintage Romantik-305 radio receiver, featuring a rectangular black plastic and metal body with a prominent carrying handle on top. The front panel displays a cassette tape deck, a frequency tuning window, several control knobs and buttons, and a large speaker grille. The left side is mostly plain, while the back is vented for heat dissipation and has access panels, likely for batteries or servicing. The right side is also plain except for a small metallic detail near the bottom. The radio sits on a colorful illustrated map, which is not part of the device but provides a playful base. The overall design is utilitarian, with clear Soviet-era styling and Cyrillic labeling.
description: A black, boxy Romantik-305 radio receiver with a handle, cassette deck, speaker grille, and control knobs, featuring Cyrillic labels and a vintage design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cassette deck, speaker grille, control knobs, tuning window, and brand label.
description_view_1: Left side view, mostly plain with a few buttons and the handle visible on top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying vented panels and access doors, with no controls or branding.
description_view_3: Right side view, plain except for a small metallic detail near the bottom and the handle on top.
